title: carya integrifoliolata
intro: Carya integrifoliolata, also known as the black hickory, is a deciduous tree species native to eastern North America. The medicinal properties of black hickory are derived from its bark, which contains flavonoids, tannins, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of black hickory include its potential to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The herbal preparations of black hickory typically involve making teas, tinctures, or infusions from its bark, which are used to promote overall well-being and support immune function.
